toolchain_notes: Python 3.14 + anyio plugin autoload + Windows = OSError [WinError 10106] at asyncio backend init. Workaround: PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1 + explicit -p no:debugging -p no:cacheprovider. Separate follow-up slice should pin pytest plugins or add pyproject.toml pytest config.

open_risks: origin/main and codex/job-catalog-foundation have unrelated histories. Slice 1 shipped via the private integration lane to avoid a destructive reconciliation under pressure. Op 2 (OSS unification) must decide merge-vs-reset before any PR targets main directly.
specs/job-claims-model.md 'First implementation slice' items 1-6 are drifted vs. code (already implemented) and should be reconciled in a follow-up docs-hygiene slice after PR #90 lands.
Python 3.14 + anyio plugin autoload breaks pytest on Windows; validation depends on a workaround. A follow-up slice should add a pytest config or pin plugins.
Upstream integration strategy for the 3 forked repos is not yet captured in repo docs; Op 2 should surface the integration boundary before code-level reconciliation.
needs_founder_decision: Op 2 path selection: (A) merge --allow-unrelated-histories to preserve public main history, or (B) archive main as refs/tags/oss-main-pre-unify and force-push the real codebase to main. User lean appears to be (B) since paid/multi-user work has not yet started and the private lane is effectively the intended OSS state.

Upstream fork integration model: which 3 forks, what role each plays (vendored dependency vs submodule vs fork-with-patches), and how they feed into the JobPipe runtime. Needed before Op 2 can be scoped concretely.
